key_insight: ILLUME+ is a scalable explainable-AI framework that goes beyond single-gene rankings to uncover more stable, biologically meaningful patterns behind cancer drug response prediction.
clinical_relevance: By revealing drug-gene associations and interaction-driven signals, this approach may improve the interpretability of precision-oncology models and support hypothesis generation for targeted therapy research.
method: Post-hoc explainability framework integrated into an end-to-end drug-response prediction pipeline
Produces multiple complementary explanation types instead of only univariate gene importance scores
Designed to improve stability and capture coordinated gene activity linked to sensitivity and resistance
evidence_signal: According to the abstract, ILLUME+ generated more stable gene-importance scores than baseline explainers and recovered known drug-gene mechanisms while also surfacing novel interaction-based hypotheses.
watch_item: As a post-hoc explanation method, its biological insights still depend on the quality of the underlying transcriptomic data and predictive model, and extra validation is needed before any translational use.
paper_title: Explainable AI for Cancer Drug Response Prediction: Beyond Univariate Feature Attributions
